features: second-level reseller wallet is charged at the per-GB rate configured by the master reseller
the master wallet receives the collected second-level usage income as a separate ledger entry
the master wallet is independently charged at the central per-GB rate for the same Marzban traffic
PasarGuard settlement runs child collection before central panel billing so the cascade order is deterministic
child debt and master upstream debt are tracked independently and may suspend their own level independently
central reports separate child gross billing, master income, central upstream cost, debt, and accrued profit
financial events are guarded by idempotent event keys and D1 transaction batches

deployment.automatic_provisioning: upload ZIP through the central bot

validation.fresh_sqlite_schema: passed: shared cascade columns and indexes
validation.legacy_schema_migration: passed: additive D1 columns are idempotent
validation.placeholder_bind_validation: passed: 24 prepared statements in each Worker billing path
validation.financial_order: passed: child debit -> master income -> master central debit
validation.duplicate_event_guard: passed by event-key and processing-token design
validation.live_api: not run; requires real Telegram, PasarGuard and Marzban credentials

limitations: Existing historical Marzban usage is not retroactively re-billed; cascading settlement starts from the next measured delta.
PasarGuard central cost is still sourced from the real manager aggregate; child attribution is shown separately without double-debiting the master.
